element_introduction_order.first_appears: Inherited from first game, tutorial context

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: New directional variant enabling vertical enemy engagement

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Inherited from first game, early acts
Throughout each act (4 letters per act)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Unlocks cup-switching bonus game for extra lives
Reward exploration and drill jump mastery
Temporary advantage mechanics throughout level progression

typical_relief_triggers: Safe platforming sections between enemy clusters
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy encounters requiring drill jump precision
Secret area hunting (risk of missing letters)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exploration-paced with combat punctuation; significantly longer acts than first game allow for rhythm modulation through secret-seeking vs. direct pathing

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden letter placement and secret area concealment through obscured pathways; exploration encouraged but not required for progression
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: AERO letters hidden throughout acts; secret areas accessible via lateral exploration and precision drill jump techniques

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduces drill jump variants and enemy patterns; establishes secret-seeking as parallel objective
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Acts become significantly longer; secret density increases; enemy combinations grow complex
notable_spikes: World transitions introduce visual/thematic complexity via ancient castle setting
Late acts require sustained precision in drill jump aiming (diagonal/vertical/straight-down variants)

notable_level_patterns.description: All 8 worlds divided into 3 acts each (final world varies), with exit-finding as primary goal. Acts designed with multiple secret areas and four hidden letters spelling AERO.
Levels feature numerous secret areas accessible through exploration and precision movement; described as 'ingeniously designed' with rewards for thorough play
Drill jump variants (diagonal, straight-down) enable vertical traversal and combat, allowing level designs to use multi-layer height variation
